_readme: Soft-warn thresholds for data entry. DAVID OWNS THESE NUMBERS.
Crossing soft_min/soft_max shows 'is that right?' — it NEVER blocks; confirmed values save and are logged.
hard_min is the only block (0 = negative impossible). Never add a hard plausibility cap here.
monotonic:'seniority' warns when a junior level beats a senior one (bonus/LTI/pension/notice only).
max_of:<qid> warns when this 'maximum' sits below its typical/target twin on the same level.
Edits hot-reload (no restart). Re-running seed_validation_config.py REGENERATES this file from the library rules and overwrites manual edits.

323ffcf1-749b-43f3-bf34-1de6b8b1ca67.label: Maximum bonus % opportunity by level
323ffcf1-749b-43f3-bf34-1de6b8b1ca67.why: exec bonus opportunity can exceed 100% of base; library cap 100 too tight

3faf1f0c-f753-497f-a395-384bba38c5e3.label: Average annual private medical insurance (PMI) premium
3faf1f0c-f753-497f-a395-384bba38c5e3.why: GBP per employee per year; generous so senior-heavy books don't false-warn

a7ed418e-b057-4b70-ab58-31e897b7c1b6.label: Total annual payment for each allowance
a7ed418e-b057-4b70-ab58-31e897b7c1b6.why: GBP per year; covers London weighting / on-call at the generous end

ATT_HIR_AGENCY_RATE_01.label: Agency usage rate by level
ATT_HIR_AGENCY_RATE_01.why: a percentage share; >100 is almost surely a typo — but still only a warn

ATT_HIR_CPH_01.why: a percentage share; >100 is almost surely a typo — but still only a warn

GRO_LEA_LD_SPEND_01.why: seeded from library hard_max (now a warn, never a block)

REW_BEN_112.label: Typical employer pension contribution by level
REW_BEN_112.why: UK employer/employee pension rarely >30% but possible; library cap 100 uselessly loose as a warn

REW_BEN_FLEX_ALLOW_01.label: Annual flexible benefits allowance by level % of base pay
REW_BEN_FLEX_ALLOW_01.why: % of base; flex pots rarely exceed 10-15%

REW_INC_111.label: Target bonus opportunity by level (as % of base salary)

REW_INC_LTI_MAX_01.label: Maximum annual LTI % of base pay by level
REW_INC_LTI_MAX_01.why: senior LTI routinely 150-300%+ of base; library cap 100 far too tight

REW_INC_LTI_VALUE_TYP_01.label: Target annual LTI % of base pay by level
REW_INC_LTI_VALUE_TYP_01.why: senior LTI routinely 150-300%+ of base; library cap 100 far too tight

REW_PAY_MKT_POS_01.label: On average, where does base salary sit versus market median by employee level? (where market is 100%) so 110% would be 10% above market
REW_PAY_MKT_POS_01.why: stores % of market median (~100); the library 0-100 cap would warn on every above-median payer
